title: T-001 — Migration: items table (universal item master)
prompt:
# T-001 — Migration: items table (universal item master)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.1, §6.1, §6.4
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Drizzle migration: items master table with RLS, indexes, item_type+status enums.

Use Drizzle migration in db/migrations. Follow pattern of existing organizations/users tables. CHECK constraints expressed as Postgres CHECK clauses. RLS policy named `items_org_isolation` on the table using `USING (org_id = app.current_org_id()) WITH CHECK (org_id = app.current_org_id())` — the foundation function from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC. uom_base accepted values informational (kg|g|l|ml|pcs) — store as TEXT (no enum lock yet). gs1_gtin nullable. Coordinate with 02-SETTINGS reference_schemas — schema_version field reserved for future ext_jsonb shape evolution. Do NOT seed any rows in this task.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_items.sql [create]
- db/schema/items.ts [create]
- tests/db/items.migration.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- BOM tables (separate task T-002)
- Cost history table (T-003)
- Allergen tables (T-004)
- API endpoints (T-010..)
- UI work

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a clean test database, when the migration runs, then table `items` exists with the explicit column set listed in this task/§5.1 (do not rely on stale column counts) and item_type/status checks per §5.1.
2. Given table `items` exists, when querying pg_indexes, then idx_items_org_type, idx_items_d365 (partial), idx_items_ext_jsonb (GIN) are present.
3. Given RLS is enabled on items, when SELECT runs as `app_user` without a prior `app.set_org_context(...)` call (so `app.current_org_id()` returns NULL), then no rows are visible.
4. Given the migration file, when re-applied, then it is idempotent (no duplicate-table error).
5. Given pg_policies is inspected, when the items policy is listed, then qual/withcheck reference `app.current_org_id()` and contain no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC.

## Test strategy
- Add failing RED test in tests/db/items.migration.test.ts before writing migration; tests connect as `app_user` and exercise both the NULL-context deny path and the post-`app.set_org_context(...)` allow path.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/items.migration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not modify organizations or users tables.
- Do not seed any item rows here.
- Do not introduce hard FK to D365 — d365_item_id is a soft reference (TEXT).
- D365 fields are optional integration references only; Monopilot item rows remain canonical after NPD Builder handoff.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.

pipeline_inputs.description: Drizzle migration: items master table with RLS (foundation app.current_org_id() function), indexes, item_type+status enums.
pipeline_inputs.details: Use Drizzle migration in db/migrations. Follow pattern of existing organizations/users tables. CHECK constraints expressed as Postgres CHECK clauses. Coordinate with 02-SETTINGS reference_schemas — schema_version field reserved for future ext_jsonb shape evolution. Do NOT seed any rows in this task. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context).
